subject: Proposal from simplifier
body: Simplifier plan-phase guardrails (P1–P7) plus the human-focused plan summary at .egg-state/drafts/pipeline-dcdad92d-plan-human.md. Derived from the finalized refine analysis (996fe06d) and the operator's HITL resolutions (8abb035f); no task_planner draft existed at assessment time, so these are the binding-input-derived constraints my plan review will apply. P1: only three real dependency chains order anything (router→lenses; finding schema→computed verdict→rendering; smoke set→wave-2 rebalance, plus R6 after edits 8+10) — any other ordering is a fake dependency and an AC-2 violation. P2: the 11 lenses are one template task plus eleven instantiations, not eleven bespoke designs; skill-auditor retirement rides the lens slices. P3: subsumptions (router/reviewer-mapper, skill-auditor/lenses) are single build-and-replace tasks, never split build+removal pairs. P4: HITL resolutions delete work — no graduation-bar tasks, blocking-verdict threshold is an implementer-chosen documented default inside R8(b), no ownership caveats, no cap-raising tasks or cap questions (R9 builds inside current caps). P5: coherent slices, not one per requirement ID (indicative grouping provided). P6: R14 and R17 are near-zero-cost riders (doc line in R11's slice; render path on R8(c)), not slices. P7: §6 interface guarantees attach as acceptance criteria to the slices that could break them — neither standalone verify-tasks nor omission.

payload.risk_considered: Over-cutting risk avoided: every guardrail preserves the operator-mandated scope (all 11 lenses, launch defaults, full eval suite built this run); P-findings constrain plan shape, not scope. Under-constraining risk: P5's grouping is explicitly indicative so the task_planner keeps slicing authority. Double-build risks pre-flagged for the plan: bespoke-lens explosion (P2), split subsumption tasks (P3), resurrected HITL work (P4), riders promoted to slices (P6).

subject: heartbeat: PROPOSED
body: simplifier plan proposal v1 (a71070e) recorded; awaiting reviewer_plan; will review task_planner/architect drafts against P1-P7 when they propose

subject: ACK from reviewer_plan for simplifier
body: Plan-phase simplification guardrails (P1-P7) + human summary are directive-compliant and sound. P1 names exactly the 3 real build-dependency chains (router->lenses; finding schema->computed verdict->rendering; smoke set->wave-2 rebalance) plus the R6-after-edits-8/10 rider, matching operator direction 3, and correctly labels any roadmap-phase/eval-gated ordering as a fake dependency (AC-2). P2/P3 are legitimate reuse guardrails (one lens template + 11 instantiations; unified build-and-replace subsumption tasks). P4 correctly removes HITL-deleted work per persisted resolution 8abb035f (no graduation-bar tasks; blocking threshold = documented implementer default, not a HITL/slice; no ownership/at-risk framing; no cap-raising tasks or cap questions). P7 treats the six §6 consumer interface guarantees as acceptance criteria on the slices that could break them, not consumer-repo PRs or standalone verify-tasks (operator direction 6). Human summary is accurate, correctly stamped with pipeline-dcdad92d (no stale-id issue), and honestly flagged as pre-slice-list. No scope removed, no fake dependencies introduced, no consumer-repo targets, no resurrected HITL work, no §3/#194 re-implementation. ACK.

No scope removed, no fake dependencies introduced, no consumer-repo targets, no resurrected HITL work, no §3/#194 re-implementation. ACK.

subject: Proposal from risk_analyst
body: Plan-phase risk analysis for pipeline-dcdad92d: 21 risks (RK-1..RK-21) across sequencing/dependency ordering, scope & boundary, correctness/regression, trust-boundary/security, cost/capacity, eval-suite-as-project, and gh-aw engine dimensions. Each risk carries likelihood/impact and a plan-encodable mitigation (slice ordering, acceptance criterion, or review tripwire). Delivers 7 risk-derived plan-phase acceptance gates: (1) R5 smoke harness blocks R7 wave-2 rebalance, router blocks lenses, R8a schema blocks R8b verdict+R17 (genuine build deps, not eval gates); (2) one shared eval harness with smoke as a tagged subset, R14 reuses R11's version stamp; (3) all 11 lenses as build tasks with security&auth as one lens (split=HITL), no roadmap/eval deferral; (4) untrusted-input acceptance on completeness lens + E3, safe-output stays with orchestrator; (5) R8 determinism-boundary tripwires both directions; (6) §6 interface requirements as acceptance on trigger/severity/thumbs/version slices, no consumer-repo target; (7) one budget-scaling rule + one floor, caps documented not gated. Respects all operator HITL resolutions. Folds simplifier S2-S6 guardrails. Structured output at .egg-state/agent-outputs/pipeline-dcdad92d-risk_analyst-output.json.

Each risk carries likelihood/impact and a plan-encodable mitigation (slice ordering, acceptance criterion, or review tripwire). Respects all operator HITL resolutions. Folds simplifier S2-S6 guardrails. Structured output at .egg-state/agent-outputs/pipeline-dcdad92d-risk_analyst-output.json.

attestation.no_decisions_rationale: No new HITL decisions this phase. The four proposal open questions were resolved by the operator at the refine gate (graduation bar=post-launch, blocking-verdict threshold=implementer judgment+documented default, vendor-vs-consume=consume, ownership=determined) and the budget/timeout caps were explicitly resolved as non-blocking with documented defaults; directive 7 forbids re-raising these. The one conditional HITL trigger in scope (security&auth lens split, simplifier S2) is not active — the plan builds exactly 11 lenses and only raises HITL if implementation surfaces a genuine seam. No plan-phase build choice currently depends on an unresolved operator question, so no decision is registered.

payload.risk_considered: Highest-impact risks: RK-1 (wave-2 ships before smoke set = undetected recall regression), RK-5 (R8 determinism boundary grows into judgment = most likely over-build), RK-13 (completeness lens Jira/Confluence fetch = main injection surface), RK-11/RK-19 (consumer interface regression / accidental consumer-repo write). Directive-compliance risks RK-4/RK-18 (roadmap or eval-evidence deferral smuggled back as slice ordering) flagged as hard reviewer-reject conditions.

subject: ACK from reviewer_plan for risk_analyst
body: Plan-phase risk analysis (21 risks RK-1..RK-21 + 7 risk-derived acceptance gates) is directive-compliant and well-scoped. Directive 1/2: RK-4 and RK-18 make "all 11 lenses are build tasks" and "no roadmap/eval-evidence deferral" hard slice-acceptance gates and explicitly state R11 (eval suite) is never a predecessor to lens/prompt construction — only the shared-harness->smoke-CI ordering is legitimate regression protection. Directive 3: RK-1/2/3 name exactly the three genuine build chains (R5 smoke->R7 wave-2 rebalance; router->lenses; R8a finding schema->R8b computed verdict + R17), matching the approved analysis and simplifier P1; no fake dependencies introduced. Directive 4: RK-16/RK-17 keep first-principles on Fable 5 advisory-only day one and Fable per-role arms as post-suite experiment notes (not build slices), and centralize the effort/model table. Directive 5: RK-9 makes AC-1 a per-slice check citing the #194 baseline. Directive 6: RK-11/RK-19 treat §6 interface requirements as slice acceptance criteria and forbid any consumer-repo target. Directive 7: RK-6 makes the security-lens split a HITL question, §8 records operator-resolved questions without re-raising them, and decisions_registered is empty with a sound rationale. Correctly folds simplifier S2-S6 (RK-5/S5, RK-6/S2, RK-7/S3, RK-8/S4, RK-15/S6). No resurrected HITL-deleted work; mitigations are all plan-encodable (ordering, acceptance criteria, review tripwires). Non-blocking cosmetic nit: the JSON's self-referential commit_sha field reads db7f6cbc rather than the proposal SHA. ACK.

subject: Proposal from task_planner
body: Plan artifact pipeline-dcdad92d-plan.md (re-propose after fixing role<->file alignment). Decomposes the operator-approved refine analysis (R1-R17) into 12 build slices / 56 tasks on a linear implementation spine (S1->S2->S3->S4->S5->S6->S7->S9->S10->S11->S12; thumbs S8 file-disjoint off S1), forced by the #3046 no-overlap rule since workflows/review/review.md is touched by 8 slices. Fixes in this revision: split TASK-9-3 (tester keeps smoke.test.ts gated by existing pnpm-test CI) from new TASK-9-4 (coder stages the CI workflow under .github-staging/ per #2508, no producer role may push .github/ directly); reassigned TASK-10-4 (R6 experiment note) tester->documenter. The three operator-named build edges hold as real edges: schema(S1)->verdict(S2), router(S3)->lenses(S7), smoke(S9)->wave-2(S10). All 11 lenses are build tasks (TASK-7-1..7-11; security a single xhigh lens with a pre-declared implement-time HITL split trigger). Model launch defaults are build tasks (TASK-6-3); Fable arms post-suite notes only. No roadmap/eval-gated deferral; no consumer-repo deliverables (interface guarantees section 4). #194/section-3 guarded build-upon. Architect's merged section 8 finalizes code siting to actions/review/* and the gh-aw single-session bash/JSON invocation, converging without re-ordering. Validated: plan parser, preflight, #3046 overlap check, and role-allowlist check across all 56 tasks all clean, 0 warnings.

attestation.no_decisions_rationale: No HITL decision is registered at plan time: the four proposal open questions were resolved by the operator at the refine gate (graduation bar not considered this run; blocking-verdict threshold delegated to implementer judgment with a documented default in TASK-2-1, tunable later, explicitly not a gate; vendor-vs-consume resolved to consume; ownership determined). Budget/timeout caps are not surfaced per the operator HITL resolution. The one implement-time judgment call (a potential security-lens split along the risk-config seam) is pre-declared in TASK-7-1 as an implement-phase HITL trigger, not a plan-time decision, per operator direction 7 and simplifier guardrail 2. No build choice in this decomposition depends on an unresolved judgment call.

subject: ACK from risk_analyst for task_planner
body: ACK. The plan is strongly risk-aligned; all six of my hard reviewer-reject conditions are clear. (1) All 11 specialist lenses are unconditional build tasks (S7 task-7-1..7-11); no lens is roadmap-phased or eval-evidence-gated (RK-4/RK-18 clear, AC-4/AC-2 met). (2) One eval harness — the smoke set is a tagged subset of the S11 corpus with a shared loader/runner (RK-7 clear); one drift surface — the S11 version stamp is the single consumer-readable hash, R14 documents it and adds no mechanism (RK-8 clear). (3) The determinism boundary stays plumbing-only: §0 states it, and §8.7 makes the converse tripwire mechanically checkable (any actions/review/* CLI emitting a human-read sentence or scoring beyond severity/confidence = reject) (RK-5 clear). (4) Zero consumer-repo targets; every consumer-named item is a §4 interface guarantee, and the webapp #40536 causal experiment runs via the no-post harness writing only into this repo (RK-19 clear, AC-3 met). (5) S10 wave-2 rebalance depends on S9 smoke set — genuine regression protection, not an eval gate (RK-1 clear); router S3 precedes lenses S7 (RK-2); schema S1 precedes verdict S2 (RK-3) — the three operator-named build edges (AC-2) all hold. (6) Security & auth is a single xhigh lens with an explicit HITL-if-split trigger, never a unilateral split (RK-6 clear). Remaining risks are structurally mitigated: R2 core-vs-note-and-continue dimension gate is enumerated (task-2-2, RK-10); safe-output calls stay solely with the orchestrator and code never posts (§8.4/§8.7, RK-14); completeness reviewer's Jira/Confluence fetch is confined to the non-posting sub-agent with fetched text treated as data under review, backed by the E3 untrusted-input rule (task-6-1/task-4-1, RK-13); first-principles is advisory-only/never-blocks on Fable 5 (RK-16); the gh-aw single-session constraint is resolved by invoking deterministic TS CLIs via bash on JSON scratch files (§8, RK-21); overfitting guards + adversarial hard gate are in task-11-4 (RK-20). Two NON-BLOCKING notes for the implement phase (neither alters slicing, so not a NACK): RK-12 — add a targeted test of the R1 single-submission call/body shape, since no-post mode (§8.5) stubs the only side-effecting boundary and won't exercise it; RK-13 — extend S9's adversarial-injection cases to specifically cover the Jira/Confluence ticket-fetch path, not just PR-body injection. HITL status is correct: the four proposal open questions are resolved and non-blocking per the operator's refine-gate resolution, and the plan re-raises none of them (no directive-7 violation).

subject: ACK from reviewer_plan for task_planner
body: Full implementation plan (12 slices + Structured Task Appendix) is sound and directive-compliant on all five acceptance criteria. AC-1: §7 traces every requirement R1..R17 to a slice/task; all 13 remaining prompt edits are present (edit 4 = #194 excluded); §0 makes "consume #194, never re-implement §3" a per-slice reject rule and task-2-1/6-3/6-4 explicitly extend rather than re-pin. AC-2: the three operator-named build edges are honored (finding schema S1 -> computed verdict S2; router S3 -> lenses S7; smoke set S9 -> wave-2 rebalance S10); the linear spine's additional edges are transparently labeled as #3046 same-file integration serialization (review.md is touched by 8 slices, forcing a linear chain) rather than roadmap-phase or eval-evidence deferral, with each slice's genuine upstreams documented separately as transitive ancestors, and nothing deferred out of scope. AC-3: no consumer-repo target anywhere — all consumer-named work is §4 interface guarantees (thumbs sweep no consumer commit, #40536 experiment via no-post harness, eval datasets read consumer records without writing, CI staged under .github-staging/ per #2508). AC-4: S7 task-7-1..7-11 build all 11 named lenses; security & auth is a single xhigh lens with a pre-declared HITL-if-split trigger, not a unilateral 12th. AC-5: task-6-3 builds the Opus-4.8 workhorse + per-role effort table + first-principles on Fable 5 day one; §5 keeps per-role Fable/Sonnet arms as post-suite experiment notes with no slice constructing them. Simplifier guardrails and risk-analyst gates are honored: P4 clean (no graduation-bar/ownership/cap-raising tasks; blocking threshold is a documented implementer default in task-2-1), P3 subsumptions land within one slice (router+reviewer-mapper-drop in S3; skill-auditor fold-in in S7), P6 R14/R17 are riders (task-12-2 doc-only pointing at the single version stamp; task-12-5 renders from the existing pre_merge_obligation field), P7 §4 interface requirements are cross-cutting ACs not verify-task theater, and there is one shared eval harness (S9/S11), one version/drift surface (task-11-5), and one budget rule + floor (task-3-2). RK-1/2/3/5/6/7/13/19 satisfied. P2's shared lens shape is captured at the slice level (all 11 lenses live in review.md under a uniform tri-state-hunt + schema-conformance AC), which is substantively compliant — not eleven bespoke designs. No resurrected HITL-deleted work, no fake deferral, no consumer targets, no §3 re-implementation. One non-blocking consistency item recorded as a pre-merge condition: reconcile the yaml appendix code paths (workflows/review/lib/*.ts) with §8.3's finalized siting (actions/review/*.ts) so modules are created at the tsc/vitest-wired path; this is architect-owned and already has a documented name-for-name mapping, so it does not block the decomposition. ACK.

Non-blocking on the plan decomposition; architect-owned, name-for-name mapping already documented.

subject: Proposal from architect
body: Architect plan-phase deliverables: (1) the authoritative forest slice scaffold at .egg-state/agent-outputs/pipeline-dcdad92d-architect-slices.yaml, and (2) the code-integration/siting decision the task_planner deferred (plan §8), plus architect-output.json. Scaffold: the task_planner's converged 12-slice/51-task spine reproduced verbatim (tasks, files, dependencies), with the architect certifying the forest and adding serialized_chain_order recording each slice's genuine upstream deps (analysis DAG) distinct from the linear-spine edge. Verified against egg_contracts.plan_parser: VALID forest (each slice ≤1 parent) and NO slice-overlap violation (#3046). Spine S1→S2→S3→S4→S5→S6→S7→S9→S10→S11→S12 with S8 (thumbs, file-disjoint) parallel off S1; the linear spine is forced by workflows/review/review.md being edited by eight slices (sibling branches would collide at integration). The three operator-named edges are honored as real build deps: schema(S1)→verdict(S2), router(S3)→lenses(S7), smoke(S9)→rebalance(S10). Independently reached the same spine as the task_planner (strong convergence). Siting decision (§8): review-internal support code lives in the self-contained workflows/review/ package (workflows/review/lib/ + workflows/review/eval/), NOT actions/ — actions/ is reserved for action.yml-bearing reusable composite actions consumed via uses:, which this code is not; fixed the one stale actions/review/* reference in the forest-note accordingly. Integration mechanism: TS CLI tools invoked by the orchestrator via bash (node -r @swc-node/register) over JSON in the existing /tmp/gh-aw/review/ scratch dir; orchestrator stays the single agentic session and sole safe-output caller. Three surfaces named (in-session CLIs / standalone scheduled workflows / no-post eval harness); in-session data-flow contract extending #194's on-disk convention (routing.json → schema-validate → verdict.json → render → existing safe outputs; malformed lens output feeds the R2 missing-dimension gate); no-post run mode as a one-place safe-output stub (R5/R6/R11 mechanism); router's single model touch kept outside the deterministic core; boundary-enforcement wiring making the R8 converse tripwire mechanically checkable. No #194 re-implementation; consumers interface-only; trigger overrides preserved (no on:/label frontmatter touched).

payload.summary: Architect plan-phase deliverables: (1) the authoritative forest slice scaffold at .egg-state/agent-outputs/pipeline-dcdad92d-architect-slices.yaml, and (2) the code-integration/siting decision the task_planner deferred (plan §8), plus architect-output.json. Scaffold: the task_planner's converged 12-slice/51-task spine reproduced verbatim (tasks, files, dependencies), with the architect certifying the forest and adding serialized_chain_order recording each slice's genuine upstream deps (analysis DAG) distinct from the linear-spine edge. Verified against egg_contracts.plan_parser: VALID forest (each slice ≤1 parent) and NO slice-overlap violation (#3046). Spine S1→S2→S3→S4→S5→S6→S7→S9→S10→S11→S12 with S8 (thumbs, file-disjoint) parallel off S1; the linear spine is forced by workflows/review/review.md being edited by eight slices (sibling branches would collide at integration). The three operator-named edges are honored as real build deps: schema(S1)→verdict(S2), router(S3)→lenses(S7), smoke(S9)→rebalance(S10). Independently reached the same spine as the task_planner (strong convergence). Siting decision (§8): review-internal support code lives in the self-contained workflows/review/ package (workflows/review/lib/ + workflows/review/eval/), NOT actions/ — actions/ is reserved for action.yml-bearing reusable composite actions consumed via uses:, which this code is not; fixed the one stale actions/review/* reference in the forest-note accordingly. Integration mechanism: TS CLI tools invoked by the orchestrator via bash (node -r @swc-node/register) over JSON in the existing /tmp/gh-aw/review/ scratch dir; orchestrator stays the single agentic session and sole safe-output caller. attestation.no_decisions_rationale: No HITL decision is registered at plan time. The four proposal open questions were resolved by the operator at the refine gate and are non-blocking (graduation bar: not considered; blocking-verdict threshold: documented default, tunable later, not a gate; vendor-vs-consume: consume; ownership: humans maintain). The seven 2026-07-02 directions resolved this phase's structural judgment calls. The architect's plan §8 integration/siting decision and the forest serialization are pure technical-design choices with no operator judgment required — they select among engineering options (CLI-over-bash vs multi-job; workflows/review/lib/ vs actions/ siting; linear spine to satisfy the forest + no-overlap constraints) on reuse/correctness grounds, changing no scope and no ordering of the operator-named build edges. The pre-declared security-lens-split trigger (plan §6/task-7-1) and the residual blocking-verdict threshold remain HITL-if-a-build-choice-depends per operator direction 7; neither is live now because no current build choice depends on them.

payload.risk_considered: Key architect risks addressed: (1) non-forest / multi-parent DAG — resolved via a single linear spine + S8 parallel branch; validated with egg_contracts.plan_parser validate_forest = VALID. (2) slice-overlap violation (#3046) from eight slices editing review.md + render-comment.ts shared by S2/S12 — resolved by the spine ordering all overlapping writers; validate_slice_file_overlap = NONE. (3) stacked-PR code-availability at convergence slices — the linear spine makes every real upstream a transitive ancestor, so each slice's branch carries the code it needs. (4) siting wrong-dir — analyzed that actions/* are action.yml composite actions, so review-internal code belongs in the workflows/review/ package; fixed the stale actions/review/* forest-note reference for doc consistency. (5) two-artifact divergence — regenerated the scaffold verbatim from the task_planner's converged appendix so paths/tasks/deps match exactly; the scaffold is authoritative for contract population. (6) determinism-boundary drift, no-post GitHub write, router model-in-core, trigger-override regression — all mitigated in §8 as before. No #194 re-implementation; no consumer-repo target.

subject: ACK from risk_analyst for architect
body: ACK. The architect's contribution is confined to its lane — the code-integration mechanism and siting (plan §8) plus the authoritative forest slice scaffold — and it preserves every risk mitigation I care about; none of my six hard reviewer-reject conditions fires. (1) The slices.yaml reproduces the task_planner's converged 12-slice/51-task spine verbatim (tasks, files, dependencies), certifies a valid forest with no #3046 slice-overlap violation, and adds serialized_chain_order recording genuine upstreams — no lens dropped, roadmap-phased, or eval-gated (RK-4/RK-18 clear, AC-4 met). (2) The three operator-named build edges are explicitly honored (dependency_edges_honored) with dag_convergence certifying no re-ordering: schema S1→verdict S2, router S3→lenses S7, smoke S9→wave-2 S10 (RK-1/2/3 clear). (3) The determinism-boundary converse tripwire is preserved, merely repointed from actions/review/* to workflows/review/lib/* (§8.7): any CLI emitting a human-read sentence or scoring beyond severity/confidence remains a mechanically-checkable review reject (RK-5 clear). (4) consumer_repos_interface_only:true and no_194_reimplementation:true are unchanged; no consumer-repo target introduced (RK-19 clear, AC-3 met). (5) One shared finding-schema.ts imported by all three surfaces and one version-stamp surface — no second eval harness or drift mechanism (RK-7/RK-8 clear). (6) Security & auth lens unchanged as single xhigh lens with HITL-if-split (RK-6 clear). The one substantive change — reversing code siting from actions/review/ to workflows/review/lib/ (+ workflows/review/eval/) with node -r @swc-node/register invocation — is a sound technical-design call: I verified its reuse premise is factually true (@swc-node/register is a root devDependency, vitest.config.ts has no include override so it discovers workflows/review/lib/*.test.ts by default, and workflows/review/package.json confirms a self-contained package), and it coherently resolves the earlier actions/-vs-appendix path inconsistency by endorsing the appendix paths name-for-name with no change to roles/tasks/ordering. Trigger-override integrity is preserved by construction (Surface-A bash calls touch no on:/label frontmatter, §8.8). One NON-BLOCKING note for implement (reviewer_plan/architect lane, not a NACK): runtime invocation via `node -r @swc-node/register *.ts` requires @swc-node/register resolvable at workflow runtime, not just under vitest — the architect already hedged this with "or a pre-bundled entry," so it is an implement-time detail, not a slicing risk. HITL status remains correct: no re-raise of the four operator-resolved open questions (no directive-7 violation).

subject: ACK from reviewer_plan for architect
body: Architect proposal (slices.yaml scaffold + output.json integration decision + plan §8 rewrite) is sound, decomposition-faithful, and directive-compliant. It resolves the one deferred technical-design decision — code-integration mechanism and siting in the gh-aw single-session model — landing on TypeScript CLI tools under workflows/review/lib/ (+ workflows/review/eval/), invoked by the orchestrator via bash (node -r @swc-node/register) over JSON in the existing /tmp/gh-aw/review/ scratch dir, with the orchestrator remaining the single session and sole safe-output caller. This RESOLVES the path discrepancy I conditionally flagged on the task_planner: rather than relocating to actions/, the architect endorses the appendix paths name-for-name and corrects the earlier §8.3 claim that "workflows/ has no TS build wired." I verified that correction against the repo: the root vitest config has no restrictive include (so vitest's default glob discovers **/*.test.ts under workflows/ and transforms .ts natively without a separate build), @swc-node/register is a devDependency, and workflows/review/ is already a self-contained package with its own package.json — so workflows/review/lib/*.ts is genuinely buildable and testable, and the rejection of actions/ (reserved for action.yml-bearing reusable composite actions consumed via uses:) on cohesion grounds is well-reasoned. plan §8.1/8.3/8.4/8.7/8.8 are all consistently updated to workflows/review/lib/. The slices.yaml reproduces the task_planner's converged 12-slice decomposition verbatim (tasks, files, roles, single-parent dependencies), certifies a valid forest with no #3046 slice-overlap violation against egg_contracts.plan_parser, and adds a serialized_chain_order field that records each slice's genuine analysis-DAG upstreams distinct from the linear-spine serialization edge. All five acceptance criteria remain satisfied: all 11 lenses are build tasks (TASK-7-1..7-11; security xhigh single lens with the pre-declared HITL-if-split trigger), model launch defaults are a build task (TASK-6-3) with Fable arms only as post-suite notes, no consumer-repo target appears (all paths under workflows/review/* or .github-staging/), every requirement still traces to a slice, and #194/§3 is consumed not re-implemented. The three operator-named build edges (schema->verdict, router->lenses, smoke->rebalance) hold with no re-ordering. decisions_registered is empty with a sound rationale — the siting/mechanism choice is a pure technical-design decision on reuse and cohesion grounds, changing no scope or ordering, so it correctly requires no operator HITL (directive 7 respected; the security-lens-split and residual blocking-threshold triggers remain HITL-if-a-build-choice-depends). One non-blocking cosmetic leftover: plan.md line 58 still carries the task_planner's original indicative "or a new actions/review-*" placeholder, now superseded by §8.3's confirmation. ACK.
